commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer label, security-sensitive tool execution behavior, compatibility impact, and missing real behavior proof make this a human review path rather than an automated repair candidate.; Cleared: The diff tightens an exec approval security boundary and does not add dependencies, workflow changes, credential handling, or new code-execution supply-chain surface. commands.review_summary:
The PR changes exec wrapper resolution and allowlist analysis so BSD/macOS `script` transcript wrappers and GNU `time -o/--output` wrappers are policy-blocked, propagates analysis platform, and adds regression tests.

PR surface: Source +82, Tests +96. Total +178 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows `script` and `time` are still transparent dispatch wrappers, and the PR adds focused tests that would exercise the bypass path; I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Protected `maintainer` label, security-sensitive tool execution behavior, compatibility impact, and missing real behavior proof make this a human review path rather than an automated repair candidate.

target.title: fix: block side-effecting command wrappers [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Treat side-effecting dispatch wrapper forms as policy-blocked instead of transparent when evaluating exec approval allowlists.
- Keep plain GNU `time` usage transparent when it only reports to stderr, while blocking output-file forms before allowlist matching.
- Carry the requested analysis platform into command resolution so BSD/macOS wrapper behavior is tested deterministically from Linux.
- AI-assisted.

## Linked context

No public issue linked in this PR body.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Exec approval allowlists no longer approve an inner command while preserving a side-effecting wrapper for execution.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout with Node 24.15.0 through the repo Vitest wrapper.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-wrapper-resolution.test.ts src/infra/exec-command-resolution.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: `Test Files 3 passed (3); Tests 269 passed (269)`
- Observed result after fix: BSD/macOS `script` transcript wrappers and GNU `time -o` wrappers are policy-blocked; plain `time -p` remains allowlist-transparent.
- What was not tested: Full suite, cross-OS runtime execution on macOS/FreeBSD, and GitHub CI are still pending.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The BSD/macOS behavior is covered by platform-parameterized unit tests in this Linux worktree.
- Before evidence: The previous behavior reduced wrapper commands to the inner command for allowlist matching while preserving the raw source command for execution.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

`git diff --check`

`pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/infra/dispatch-wrapper-resolution.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-allowlist.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.ts src/infra/exec-wrapper-trust-plan.ts src/infra/exec-wrapper-resolution.test.ts src/infra/exec-command-resolution.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts`

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-wrapper-resolution.test.ts src/infra/exec-command-resolution.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Dispatch wrapper trust-plan tests for BSD/macOS `script` transcript wrappers and GNU `time -o` / `--output` wrappers.
- Command-resolution tests proving file-writing wrappers stay on the policy boundary.
- Allowlist tests proving the reported wrapper classes no longer satisfy an inner `git` allowlist while plain `time -p` still does.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The wrapper allowlist behavior could approve the reduced inner command while execution preserved the raw wrapper command.

If no test was added, why not?

Not applicable.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

What is the highest-risk area?

Exec approval compatibility for users who intentionally wrapped allowlisted commands with transcript or output-file wrappers.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change is limited to wrapper policy transparency, preserves non-file-output `time` behavior, and adds focused regression coverage for blocked and still-transparent cases.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Run advisory dry-run and review gates, then monitor CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Advisory gate, review gates, and GitHub CI are pending.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet.
